notes: This skill is user-invoked (disable-model-invocation), so no trigger optimization is done; the pipeline's own mechanical acceptance = the two suites scripts/run-fixtures.mjs (20) + scripts/run-injections.mjs (32). The evals in this file test whether a fresh agent, given only SKILL.md, can operate the pipeline and observe the hard-stop discipline. `files` resolve relative to the evals/ directory (input material in evals/fixtures/; the skill directory stays self-contained and portable); eval-2/3 depend on eval-1's artifact chain, and eval-3's feedback.json targetIds must first be replaced with the short IDs from eval-2's actual artifact (see its _fixtureNote).

evals.prompt: Use mini-app-spec to produce an executable spec for "add a watermark toggle to the export settings page": the toggle defaults to on, turning it off exports images without the watermark, and the toggle state must be remembered across sessions; no design mock yet. The requirement text is in req.md.
evals.expected_output: Generate mini-app-spec.html under docs/mini-app-spec/ via new/save (no hand-written HTML), register sources, decompose flows with risk tiers, validate 0 error; then stop at 🛑 Hard Stop ① and report the flow list for user confirmation — must not decompose flows and push ahead on its own

evals.prompt: Continue the spec at docs/mini-app-spec/watermark-toggle: the user has confirmed the flow list and tiering; build out the first high-uncertainty flow's states/prototype/scenarios for my review.
evals.expected_output: First status to restore context; extract→save incrementally updates the same html (revision increments, artifact not rebuilt), live-screen template + data-live expressing continuous interaction, open only after validate 0 error

evals.prompt: The user exported annotations as feedback.json (containing one annotation with a proposal; the user said verbally "adopt the first one, drop the second"); merge it into the docs/mini-app-spec/watermark-toggle spec and follow through.
evals.expected_output: merge-feedback merges them as proposed; the first one lands its proposal in the contract via save and gets annotate resolved, the second becomes rejected with its text preserved; validator 0 error throughout
